meta.title: Video B-roll — in-process decode
meta.notes: Self-demo of the IN-PROCESS video decode path: the `video` clip points straight at an mp4 (assets-src/luckymas-crt.mp4) and the editor decodes frames on demand with libav (avformat seek + avcodec decode + swscale→RGBA→texture) — NO proxy-extraction step. Scrub 0→12s: the CRT footage advances (boot screen → XP setup wizard) under a lower-third + vignette, with a gentle Ken Burns push-in (keyframes apply to video transform too). The decoder fills fps/frames/dims from the file on first open, so the asset is just `{type:video, uri}`. Identical in preview + export (both walk composite_frame). Setup: none beyond having the source mp4 in assets-src/ (gitignored). `--no-video-decode` forces the legacy JPEG-proxy fallback (needs tools/video-proxy.py).

c_vid1.notes: native 1920x1080 source → scale 1.0 fills the frame (the decoder reports real dims). `in`/`speed`/`loop` retime the source; the scale keyframes are a Ken Burns push over moving footage.

params.sub: libav · decoded live from the mp4 · scrub me — it moves
